title: Lemmy and Pixelfed smoke test
description:
## What
Smoke test federation with Lemmy and Pixelfed. Lemmy: test reply/boost/like only (Lemmy sends `Page` objects, not `Note` — must not 500); DM and follow are not expected to work (mark as `—`). Pixelfed: test follow/post/reply/boost/like/media/delete; note DM gaps. Document results in `docs/compat.md`. This is a smoke test, not a full compat pass — fix only 500 errors and inbox crashes.

## Why
Lemmy and Pixelfed are widely deployed. Lemmy's `Page` objects arrive in the inbox and must be silently ignored (not 500'd). Pixelfed has enough overlap with Mastodon AP that basic federation should work without major changes.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: capture live Lemmy and Pixelfed responses for actor doc and inbox POST shapes (Create Page from Lemmy, Create Note from Pixelfed); document all object types that differ from Mastodon Note; check Pixelfed's media attachment AP shape.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: ensure `Page` object type in Create activities returns 202 (not 500); add Pixelfed-specific shape tolerance if needed; update the `docs/compat.md` Lemmy and Pixelfed rows.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: add Lemmy fixture files in `tests/fixtures/lemmy/` including a Create Page activity; write a replay test asserting 202 for Create Page; add Pixelfed fixture files and replay tests.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: live Lemmy and Pixelfed instance responses captured via curl; the oracle for Lemmy Page is HTTP 202 (not 500).
- Test approach: fixture replay asserting correct HTTP status for each fixture type.

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths.
- `Page`, `Article`, `Question`, and any unknown object type in Create activities must return 202, not 500.
- Lemmy may send activities with `@context` arrays including Lemmy-specific extensions — must not error on unknown context values.
- Pixelfed media attachment AP shape may differ from Mastodon's — use optional fields and defaults.
- Never 500 on syntactically valid JSON activity with unknown object type.
close_reason: Lemmy Page type handled. Pixelfed uses standard AP. Need live instances.
